  • Reference
  • Articles
    Appendix A: The R System – A Brief Overview Create static html files for posting to GitHub Use of Code Files Chapter 1: Learning from data Chapter 2: Generalizing from models Chapter 3: Multiple linear regression Chapter 4: Exploiting the linear model framework Chapter 5: Generalized linear models and survival analysis Chapter 6: Time series models Chapter 7: Multilevel models, and repeated measures Chapter 8: Tree-based Classification and Regression Chapter 9: Multivariate data exploration and discrimination Code for Selected Figures Postscript
  • Changelog

    Code and Supplements for “A Practical Guide to Data Analysis Using R”

    Here is made available code and other supplementary content that relates to the new Cambridge University Press text, due to appear in May or June 2024:
    “A Practical Guide to Data Analysis Using R – An Example-Based Approach”,
    by John H Maindonald, W John Braun, and Jeffrey L Andrews.1

    The new text builds on “Data Analysis and Graphics Using R” (Maindonald and Braun, CUP, 3rd edn, 2010.)

    For suggestions on how to work with and use the code files, go to GettingStarted.

    Related Resources

    Solutions to selected exercises are available at
    https://jhmaindonald.github.io/https://jhmaindonald.githib.io/PGRselected

    Further links that may be of interest

    Updated version of early 2000s booklet on R

    https://jhmaindonald.github.io/usingR-Booklet

    This is now to a large extent superseded by tutorials that are available on the web. It may however be useful to some beginning R users.
